questions.question: An employee earning $52,400 receives a 10% pay cut. The following year they get a 10% raise. What is their final salary?
questions.misconception_explanation: Most people assume a 10% cut followed by a 10% raise returns to the original salary,not realizing the raise applies to the already-reduced amount

questions.question: You have 2 liters of a 30% acid solution. You pour out 1 liter and replace it with 1 liter of pure water. What is the new concentration?
questions.misconception_explanation: people think pouring out half and replacing with water halves the concentration from 30% to 10% -they forget they're only pour out 1 liter not the full 2

questions.question: A cyclist travels 30 km uphill at 10 km/h, then returns 30 km downhill at 30 km/h. What is the average speed for the entire journey?
questions.misconception_explanation: People assume you add 30 + 10 then divide by 2 not realizing average speed is total distance divided by total time

questions.question: A jacket has an original price of $200. Store A offers 30% off, then an additional 20% off the sale price. Store B offers a straight 50% off. Which is better and by how much?
questions.correct_answer: Store B is cheaper by $12 (Store A = $112, Store B = $100)
questions.misconception_answer: Both are equal (Store A = $100, Store B = $100)
questions.misconception_explanation: People assume that they are equal because both reduce by 50%

questions.question: A bag has 4 red and 7 blue marbles. You draw two marbles without replacement. What is the probability both are red?
questions.misconception_explanation: people forget the pool shrinks after first draw
